overrides.override_summary: Fast Food Act (AB 1228) may set health, safety, and employment standards including minimum wages, working hours, working conditions. Franchisor provides no training/assistance/fees related to Act compliance.
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees bear sole responsibility for compliance with potentially higher labor costs under the Fast Food Act. No franchisor support for implementation.

overrides.override_summary: Any release required for renewal or transfer will not apply to liability under the Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees retain right to pursue claims under state franchise law regardless of general releases signed at renewal or transfer.

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon insolvency might not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law, but franchisor will enforce to extent enforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: Bankruptcy/insolvency termination trigger may be limited by federal bankruptcy protections for Maryland franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in Maryland for claims arising under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees are not forced to litigate Maryland franchise law claims in Illinois.

overrides.override_summary: Claims under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law must be brought within 3 years after grant of franchise.
overrides.why_it_matters: 3-year limitations period from franchise grant; shorter than some general statutes.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor must give 90 days' notice of termination with 60 days to cure (except in specified cases).
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota franchisees get substantially more termination protection than the general FA provides (which has no specified cure period for Section 18 material breaches).

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor must give 180 days' notice of non-renewal.
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota franchisees get 6 months' advance warning before non-renewal, providing time to arrange orderly exit or sale.

overrides.override_summary: Consent to transfer will not be unreasonably withheld.
overrides.why_it_matters: Minnesota law limits franchisor discretion to block transfers, strengthening franchisee exit flexibility.

overrides.override_summary: Non-competition covenants are generally unenforceable in North Dakota. Franchisor will seek to enforce to extent enforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: North Dakota franchisees may operate competing businesses during and after the franchise term without legal exposure from the non-compete.

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law will govern claims arising under the North Dakota Franchise Investment Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchise law claims governed by ND law, not Illinois law.

overrides.override_summary: If Washington Franchise Investment Protection Act applies, its provisions prevail over inconsistent FDD/agreement terms.
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington state franchise law overrides conflicting agreement provisions for WA franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: Assurance of Discontinuance with WA Attorney General: removed no-poach provision from franchise agreement, will not enforce existing provisions, requested WA franchisees amend agreements. No fines or penalties.
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington specifically addressed the no-poach issue that is the subject of Deslandes and Turner pending antitrust litigation. WA franchisees' employees can move freely between McDonald's locations.
